_description: AI-specific security context for Cloud Explorer. Maps AI-related resource families to NIST AI RMF and OWASP LLM Top 10 controls.

controls.title: AI risk management process aligned with org risk tolerance
controls.relevance: Ensure AI services have proper authentication and authorization governance

controls.title: Intended purpose and context of use documented
controls.relevance: AI endpoints without authentication may be used outside intended scope

controls.title: Organizational risk tolerance determined
controls.relevance: Unrestricted HTTP connectors or AI skills expand attack surface beyond tolerance

controls.title: Mechanisms to mitigate AI risks are in place
controls.relevance: DLP policies, content safety filters, and access controls mitigate AI-specific risks

controls.title: Ongoing monitoring processes in place
controls.relevance: AI services require continuous monitoring for misuse, drift, and adversarial inputs

key_concerns: Authentication and authorization on AI endpoints
Monitoring for adversarial inputs and model abuse

controls.title: Prompt Injection
controls.relevance: AI services without input validation are vulnerable to direct and indirect prompt injection

controls.title: Insecure Output Handling
controls.relevance: AI outputs consumed by downstream systems without sanitization enable injection chains

controls.title: Excessive Agency
controls.relevance: AI agents with broad permissions or unauthenticated endpoints can perform unintended actions

controls.title: Excessive Agency / Insufficient Sandboxing
controls.relevance: Unmanaged environments and unrestricted AI skills without DLP create excessive agency risks

controls.title: Overreliance
controls.relevance: AI-generated content must be validated — missing content safety filters increase hallucination risk

key_concerns: Content safety and responsible AI filters

controls.title: AI risk management aligned with org policy
controls.relevance: Apps hosting AI features should follow AI governance standards

key_concerns: Apps may embed AI features requiring AI-specific security review

controls.title: Prompt Injection
controls.relevance: Web apps with AI chat features need input validation

controls.title: Excessive Agency
controls.relevance: App-hosted AI agents must enforce least-privilege tool access

key_concerns: Validate AI inputs at the application layer
Enforce tool-level permissions for AI features

controls.title: AI risk management process
controls.relevance: Service principals for AI services need proper privilege scoping

key_concerns: AI service identities should use managed identity with minimal permissions

controls.title: Excessive Agency
controls.relevance: Over-permissioned AI service identities enable excessive agency

key_concerns: Enforce least-privilege for all AI-related service principals and managed identities
